title: target-evidence-mcp server exposing evidence cards + provenance/licence ledger to agents (open-only data; research-use disclaimer)
context: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server exposing the evidence cards and provenance/licence ledger to AI agents would enable any compliant agent to query 'open, cited evidence for target X in cancer Y' with attribution and grades attached. This is both a distribution channel for the dataset and a candidate contribution/overlay deliverable if the project pivots to Open Targets/Pharos/CIViC. It must expose only open-class card content, carry the 'not medical advice' disclaimer in every tool response, and serve no patient-level data.
objective: Build a target-evidence-mcp server (MCP protocol) exposing the evidence card dataset and provenance/licence ledger as MCP tools: query by target ID/gene symbol and cancer indication, returning card content with both grade axes, provenance records (licenseClass, attribution), and the 'not medical advice' disclaimer. Serve only open-class (open/open-sa) data; enforce licence-class at the serving layer; return no patient-level data and no treatment recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP server implemented following the Model Context Protocol specification; tools exposed include at minimum: get_evidence_card(target_id, indication_id) and list_targets(indication_id)
Every tool response includes the evidence grades (evidenceQuality + evidenceMaturity), provenance records (sourceName, licenseClass, attribution), and the 'not medical advice' disclaimer

Only open-class (open or open-sa) card content is served; non-commercial (NC) and controlled-access data are excluded at the serving layer; open-sa content includes share-alike attribution obligations in the response
No patient-level data, no treatment recommendations, no per-patient clinical guidance in any tool response
MCP server is implemented in adapters/ (not in core packages) as a vendor-specific serving adapter; the core card data and licence ledger remain in the core packages
Server documentation published; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass with MCP server included
output: A merged PR adding the target-evidence-mcp server in adapters/, exposing the evidence card dataset and provenance/licence ledger as MCP tools with open-only data enforcement and 'not medical advice' disclaimer — an agent-ecosystem distribution channel for the dataset.
